info.name: text-delete
info.text: If your theory is well built, others will want to rely on it

info.name: text-delete
info.text: A good theory, like a good building, is worth much more than the inputs that went into building it

info.name: text-delete
info.text: A strong theory can welcome new layers being laid upon the top of it

info.name: text-delete
info.text: While it may no longer be as attractive as the newer and flashier competitors, both a good building and a good theory can continue to serve people in their intended purpose.

info.name: text-delete
info.text: An improperly designed theory cannot stand on its own.

info.name: text-delete
info.text: Both a theory and a building can be torn down, if need be

info.name: text-delete
info.text: Theories and arguments can be built on shaky foundations, just as with buildings

info.name: text-delete
info.text: Everyone has their own home, and they maintain their own theories with as much sentimentality

info.name: text-delete
info.text: The more ambitious a theory tries to be, the further its builder has to fall

info.name: text-delete
info.text: A weak theory is like a house of cards, and it can collapse just as a real building would.

info.name: text-delete
info.text: A good idea or explanation is worth more than the inputs that went into making it.

info.name: text-delete
info.text: Even abstract ideas can be defaced in the way that vandals might deface property
